level_signals: 3–6 years of experience in community-led growth, campus programs, developer relations, student marketing, or a startup role where you owned end-to-end programs
Expected to define the role as much as fill it

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3–6 years of experience in community-led growth, campus programs, developer relations, student marketing, or a startup role where you owned end-to-end programs
Deep familiarity with university ecosystems (entrepreneurship clubs, CS programs, design schools, hackathon culture) from having operated inside them, not just adjacent to them
Builder instincts: you've shipped something yourself, or you're obsessed with people who do
Strong operator with bias toward doing: you'd rather run the first event imperfectly than wait for the perfect playbook
Comfortable with ambiguity and excited to define the role as much as fill it

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with event logistics, cohort programs, or ambassador-style communities is a strong plus

scope.surface: Lovable Campus Leads Program — student champion recruitment, onboarding, activation, retention, and on-campus events
scope.business_model: PLG / community-led growth, B2C and prosumer / SMB
primary_metrics_inferred: Number of campus leads recruited and retained
Expansion to new schools / self-sustaining event cohorts
Product feedback pipeline from campus leads
core_responsibilities: Own the Lovable Campus Leads Program end-to-end: recruiting, onboarding, activation, and retention of student champions
Design and run the on-campus event playbook
Set expectations, keep leads activated, collect product feedback, and celebrate wins loudly
Be the person who knows each campus lead and their administration champions personally
Build the pipeline between campus leads and the broader ambassador program
Launch and grow with the right schools first
Build relationships with faculty, club leads, and accelerator staff at target schools
Own the roadmap for expanding once the first cohort produces repeatable, self-sustaining events

green_flags: Clear and specific program ownership (end-to-end Campus Leads Program)
Small, talent-dense team signals high individual impact
Role scope is explicitly defined with concrete responsibilities
JD is candid about stage ('excited to define the role as much as fill it')
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned — unclear for international candidates applying to Stockholm-based role
Location type not explicitly stated in JD body; Stockholm inferred from company description
Early-stage program with no existing playbook may mean high ambiguity and undefined success metrics

location.cities_listed (Stockholm inferred from 'building a generation-defining company from Stockholm')
seniority.level (inferred from years range and program ownership scope)

scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
ambiguities: The JD does not specify whether this role is onsite in Stockholm, hybrid, or remote-eligible. Stockholm is mentioned only in the company description. Location type treated as onsite pending confirmation.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are entirely unmentioned; marked as unclear.
